mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a win rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and triggers sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth exceeding the 2 percent threshold confirms robust economic expansion, which boosts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above the threshold fails to produce positive forward returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal a strengthening labor market, which improves consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising interest rates indicates a supply-demand mismatch that compresses profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in >50% of cases during late-cycle phases, or the predictive power disappears after adjusting for sector rotation.
